The 3 Tips
Why Are Tips Important?
“A taste of your advice”
If what you say seems like it would work
your prospect will tend to believe you when
you go over the value of your offer
Establishes that you actually are 	an authority
(Not just someone who is a good marketer)

to improve themselves

The 3 Tips
Affiliates want to send 	solid content
to their list to prevent massive
unsubscribes, as well as to provide
value for their readers
If your video is too much of a pitch without
great content, affiliates will simply
promote someone else’s product

-- 7 of 54 --

Big Mistakes in Designing Tips
– 1 –
Going into too much detail
Going into too much detail with your ,ps can make
your audience feel overwhelmed, and when
your pitch arrives, they may assume the
product/service is “too complicated”
They won’t want to buy because they
have so much to apply

-- 8 of 54 --

Big Mistakes in Designing Tips
– 2 –
Complex or Difficult Tips
Yes, your sugges,ons may actually be challenging…
even difficult, but if they are worded with too
many specifics or too many tasks, you’ll lose
your prospect to an easier solu;on
Make your ,ps 	easy to do

-- 9 of 54 --

Big Mistakes in Designing Tips
– 3 –
An Obvious Tease
This percep,on occurs when you fail
to properly open a loop
( examples to come )
Your prospect will feel cheated, will begin to
distrust you, and fall out of rapport
However, open loops, when done correctly,
accomplish what we’re wan,ng to do:
encourage the reader to conHnue watching!

If You Mess Up Your Tips?
Everything Else In Your VSL Can Fail
o 	Your conversion will drop to nothing…
o 	Your affiliates may refuse to promote you…
o 	Your viewer will think you’re disingenuous…
o 	And, your message will not get out to the world!

The Right Way Build Your Tips
Vague But Useful
Avoid too many details
Make it helpful but incomplete
Don’t worry about giving your 	best 	secrets away
( We always assume that a person who gives us
great ,ps has 	more 	that they aren’t telling us )

Example Phrases To Make

The 3 Types Of Tips
IN ORDER OF APPEARANCE IN YOUR VSL:
The Avoid Tip
The Enjoy Tip
The How-­‐To Tip
(Your Prime Benefit Pp. This is your linchpin; all
other Pps will “fail” for the user without this!)

Connecting Tips To Modalities
Why Modality = 	Avoid Tip
What If = 	Enjoy Tip
What/How-­‐To Modality = 	How-­‐To Tip

Basic Overview Of The 3 Tips
How-­‐To Tips
Your Linchpin Tip—Without this, none of your other ,ps
will be of use to your prospect
Serves as a cloaked introduc,on to
your product or service
Uses your Primary Benefit as inspira,on;
for products, this should be the driving force
behind how your primary benefit works. For services, this
should be the primary reason why your service
solves the prospect’s problem

-- 19 of 54 --

Basic Overview Of The 3 Tips
Avoid Tips
Uses The Red Light Syndrome: Avoid ,ps “scare”
your prospects, and fear breeds aBenHon
Figure out the 3 biggest mistakes your prospect is
making when trying to achieve ___________ and
pick the juiciest one for your avoid ,p
Lead-­‐in Examples:
“The number one thing you must avoid is…”
“The number one mistake all _______ make that you
absolutely must avoid…”

-- 20 of 54 --

Basic Overview Of The 3 Tips
Enjoy Tips
Uses your number two and number three benefits from
your USP (How-­‐To uses your Primary Benefit)
This is a ,p that’s fun, exci,ng, enjoyable, or just
something very posi,ve
Ask yourself: What within my benefits creates fun, excitement,
mystery, pleasure, or a posi,ve experience?
Lead-­‐in Examples:
“Here’s the number one thing you’ll enjoy when you (access benefit)…”
“A great way to have fun with (desire of prospect) is to…”

-- 22 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Step 1: The Benefit
Taken from your USP Benefits
Avoid = Second or third benefit, or problem
these benefits address
Enjoy = Second or third benefit
How-­‐To 	= Primary Benefit
(The most important of the ,ps)
Examples To Follow

-- 23 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Step 2: Why Do It?
“This is awesome if you really
want to _________”
Explains why they should listen and
what they will get out of applying the ,p
This explana,on should be two sentences
at the most (and it can be just one)
Examples To Follow

-- 24 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Step 3: What Is It?
This is the ,ps’ driving force
( the reason it works )
Focus on making this reason why
one short sentence
This is an ideal place to coin a phrase
that you can anchor to your product
Examples To Follow

-- 25 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Step 4: How To Do It
This is your step-­‐by-­‐step explana,on
of what’s entailed in the ,p
Make it 2-­‐3 steps at the most
Step Structure:
First you to this…
And here’s why you do this…
And here’s the result if you do it.
Keep this ,p to 2-­‐5 sentences
( you can go slightly over if you absolutely have to )
Examples To Follow

-- 26 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Step 5: The Result
“Once you do ( ,p ), ________
will happen…”
“If 	you do it right… more on that
in just a moment…”
Examples To Follow

-- 27 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Step 6: The Mini Dream Story
A shortened version of the reader’s
Dream Story (using 1-­‐2 sentences)
“Imagine being able to ______”
Just 	one key aspect 	the reader can relate to
Examples To Follow

-- 28 of 54 --

Tip Formula
The 3-Tip Loop
This is a breakdown of how you start
your ,ps sec,on, and the order that
you want to follow
– 	4 SecHons –
Open 	Your How-­‐To Loop
Go Into Your Avoid Tip
Go Into Your Enjoy Tip
Close Your How-­‐To Loop

-- 29 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Section 1: Open How-To Loop
An 	open loop 	is an NLP (Neuro-­‐Linguis,c
Programming) term, meaning you 	begin
to cover a subject, but then 	pause 	to con,nue
it later, using very specific language palerns

-- 30 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Section 1: Open How-To Loop
A How-­‐To Loop
“The most important ,p for (reader’s primary goal)
is primary benefit.”
“You absolutely must do this if you really want
to (what benefit does.)”
“The reason this works to (reader’s primary goal) is
because of (driving force/coined phrase).”
“I’ll tell you more about that in just a moment…”
( this is opening the loop )

-- 31 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Section 1: Open How-To Loop
Examples of How-­‐To Loop Opens
“The most important ,p I can give you if you want to lose weight
without suffering is to include your favorite foods while you do it…
Yes, even pizza, ice cream, and burgers if you like!”
“You absolutely must do this if you really want to lose weight without
suffering through depriva;on and social isola;on.”
“The reason this works to help you lose weight is based on a scien,fic
principle used exclusively in The Every Other Day Diet called
The Caloric Staggering Principle™.”
“I’ll tell you more about that in just a moment…”
( this is opening the loop )

-- 32 of 54 --

Tip Formula
Section 2: Avoid Tip
The Avoid Tip Structure
The Benefit Of Avoiding ______
Why Avoid It?
What Is It?
How To Avoid It
The Result From Avoiding It
A Mini Dream Story (Life Aner Avoiding It)

Tip Formula
Section 2: Avoid Tip
An Avoid Tip Example
“If you really want to start dropping weight faster,
you absolutely must avoid ea,ng this specific type
of food in the early to late evening. If you decrease
or even eliminate this food at night, your insulin
levels will come down and you’ll store less fat. The
food? Carbohydrates in the form of starches, like
rice, breads, potatoes, and so on.”

fats and proteins at night, so you don’t crave

The Result From Doing It

Advanced Tips For Tips
Are your ,ps supported by evidence? If so, be sure to
include that within your ,ps
Is this ,p relevant to your reader’s
greatest desires?
Is your ,p unique? Can you create a name, catch phrase,
or label for it if it is not?
Use provoca,ve and controversial ,ps
Make your ,p as tacHle as possible
( Examples: The “measure your wrist” weight loss ,p, the
“cover one eye” rela,onship ,p, the “breathe through one
nostril” relaxa,on ,p )

4 Comparisons
Comparing Your Product And Benefits
To ExisHng or Recognized Anchors

that your prospect already knows
